Task: Set up Figma file with 390x844 phone canvas and seed design tokens (Wise-inspired forest green / lime palette)
Comment: Token-first approach to speed later iteration
Learnings: Naming tokens by role (app-blue / app-surface) beats naming by hex

Task: Prototype bottom navigation (Home / Subs / Stats / Insights / Profile) shared across all screens
Comment: (H4) Consistency & standards — same nav anchors orientation
Learnings: Persistent nav reduces 'where am I' moments in tests

Task: Prototype Home screen with 4-card KPI grid above the fold (Monthly / Active / Rarely used / Renewals)
Comment: (H1) Summary metrics visible immediately on load
Learnings: KPIs above the fold answer 'should I worry?' in <1s

Task: Design SubscriptionCard component (emoji + name + category + amount + usage tag + next renewal)
Comment: (H6) Usage tag (Heavy/Moderate/Rare) removes need to recall which services are active
Learnings: Usage tag was the surprise win — reframes whole list

Task: Design contextual renewal alert row — service + days-to-renewal + last-used + Review CTA in one line
Comment: (H1) Replaces vague notification with specific actionable info
Learnings: One-line alert > multi-step notification card

Task: Build Subscriptions list skeleton with search bar and grouped categories
Comment: Lays foundation for sort/filter layers added on Day 3

Task: Define sort control affordance on Subscriptions list
Comment: (H7) Flexibility — power-user shortcut without cluttering default view

Task: Mirror prototype in React (Figma Make export) so user testing can hit real interactions
Comment: Prototype-as-code lets test sessions feel like the real product
Learnings: React-from-Figma cuts wireframe-to-tap-target gap in half

column1: Day 2 — Add & Connect Account flow + Sev 4 fixes
Task: Design Add screen with Manual / Screenshot tabs
Comment: Two paths covers manual entry and OCR receipt parsing

Task: Design Connect Account screen with providers grouped by type (Email / Bank / Card / Wallet)
Comment: Grouping reduces visual noise vs alphabetical
Learnings: Type-grouping over alphabetical — easier to scan

Task: Design Permissions Review screen with 'What we will read' + 'What we will not do' cards before authentication
Comment: (H10, H5) Fixes Sev 4 — no privacy explanation before connecting bank/email
Learnings: Bullet checks (✓/✗) read faster than legal paragraphs

Task: Design 1.5s Secure Handoff micro-state (provider icon + spinner + '256-bit encrypted' line)
Comment: Trust-signaling beat between Permissions and Scanning
Learnings: 1.5s feels intentional not laggy — under 1s feels rushed

Task: Design Scanning screen with animated progress bar + 'Usually under 30 seconds' time estimate
Comment: (H1) Fixes Sev 3 — adds time estimate to investigation progress
Learnings: Time estimate halves perceived wait

Task: Replace alarming 'We will search your digital life' copy with specific per-provider wording
Comment: (H2) Fixes Sev 3 — match real-world expectations
Learnings: Specificity > softness when handling sensitive copy

Task: Design Review Findings screen with source-status pills (Complete / Scanning / Queued)
Comment: (H1) Fixes recommendation — show source completion on results not just progress screen
Learnings: Per-source status > single global progress

Task: Rename ambiguous 'Cancel' button to 'Skip' with confirmation modal explaining consequence
Comment: (H3, H5) Fixes Sev 4 ambiguity — explicit action verb plus undo path
Learnings: 'Skip' + modal removes ambiguity better than 'Not mine'

Task: Design confidence pill with tap-to-explainer popover (80%+ / 60-79% / <60% bands)
Comment: (H10) Fixes Sev 3 — explains '95% match' which was previously unexplained
Learnings: Colour band gives quick read; popover answers depth-of-detail

Task: Design empty state for 'no subscriptions detected in this account'
Comment: (H9) Fixes recommendation — error recovery + manual-add fallback

Task: Design Done summary screen with subs-added count + 'Connect another' option
Comment: Closes the loop and offers a next step

Task: Prototype Insights screen — Potential Annual Savings hero + AI recommendations (warning / info / success) + Renewal alerts + Usage scores
Comment: Anchors AI value prop on a money number
Learnings: Dollar number at top sets tone for whole screen

Task: Prototype Profile screen — profile header + Linked Accounts + Settings sections
Comment: Standard settings pattern keeps cognitive load low

Task: Prototype Stats screen — KPI row + Spending Trend with 6M/1Y toggle + Category donut + Highest Costs bar + Detection Source
Comment: Stats becomes the analytics home so Home stays decision-focused
Learnings: Donut + bar combo reads faster than two donuts

Task: Move Monthly Budget card from Home to top of Stats
Comment: Reduces duplicate info on Home; budget belongs with analytics
Learnings: Single home for budget cleared up Home hierarchy

Task: Replace always-visible category chips with filter sheet triggered by Filter icon + add Source filter (Email / Bank / Manual)
Comment: (H7) Power filters live behind one tap instead of cluttering the toolbar
Learnings: Two-filter sheet read cleaner than 10 always-on chips

Task: Soften confidence pill from bright lime to light mint background for readability
Comment: Bright lime was reading as 'success status' not 'estimate'
Learnings: Saturation = certainty — soften for probabilistic data

Task: Hide Add / Skip buttons and confidence pill for already-tracked items in Review Findings
Comment: Removes false affordance — user cannot add what already exists
Learnings: Tracked items needed a different visual contract

Task: Design Detected-Subscription Detail screen mirroring Subscription Detail pattern with Skip / Add CTAs
Comment: Detail view gives context for low-confidence matches before committing
Learnings: 'Why we detected this' card builds trust mid-decision

Task: Add light / dark mode toggle in app shell
Comment: Token-based theming made this 30-min job
Learnings: Dark mode revealed two contrast bugs we missed in light

Task: Draft user test script with 5 task scenarios (connect Gmail / review findings / add manual sub / find spending by category / read AI insight)
Comment: Each task touches a core feature and a heuristic fix

Task: Recruit 5 participants — mix of heavy and light subscription users
Comment: Source from design Slack groups + personal network

Task: Schedule 30-min remote sessions and send calendar invites
Comment: Stagger over Tue afternoon/evening to fit Picsart day job

Task: Set up screen recording (Zoom primary + Loom backup) and verbal consent script
Comment: Recording with consent is required for analysis on Day 7

Task: Prepare debrief questionnaire — SUS scale (10 items) + 5 qualitative prompts
Comment: SUS gives benchmarkable usability score for the writeup

Task: Pilot test with 1 friend to catch script bugs before live sessions
Comment: Cheap insurance against wasted live-session time

Task: Run 5 user tests — capture task completion times + error counts + hesitation moments
Comment: Sessions back-to-back with 15-min reset gaps
Learnings: In-person session (Marcus) added alongside remote — richer behavioural data than remote alone

Task: Same-evening synthesis — top 5 findings + 3 critical fixes prioritised by severity x frequency
Comment: Synthesise while memory is fresh; batch later for writeup

Task: Identify the screen with strongest tester disagreement — A/B candidate (navigation structure)
Comment: Nav structure divergence (5-tab vs merged Analyze) chosen as A/B variable
Learnings: Disagreement on which analytics tab to use first confirmed navigation structure as the right A/B variable

Task: Write A/B hypothesis + success metric
Comment: Hypothesis: merged Analyze tab reduces navigation errors; centre + button improves Add discoverability

Task: Annotate one screenshot per participant with key reaction moment
Comment: Visual artefacts make the writeup more credible

Task: Design variant B — 4-tab nav with centre + FAB and merged Analyze screen (Stats + Insights)
Comment: Merged Analyze tab tested whether two analytics tabs caused split-attention errors
Learnings: Single Analyze tab eliminated the 'Stats or Insights first?' decision point entirely

Task: Prototype variant B in code with URL flag toggle (?v=b)
Comment: URL flag is the simplest way to serve two versions side-by-side
Learnings: AnalyzeScreen component + goTo remap in App.tsx — total change was 3 files

Task: Write A/B test participant instructions + scoped tasks
Comment: Same tasks per variant so comparison is fair

Task: Recruit 4 participants (2 per variant) with random assignment
Comment: Solo project — randomised via coin flip

Task: Build custom A/B testing platform — participant survey (/survey?v=a|b) + researcher dashboard (/results) with Supabase backend
Comment: Replaced Maze/Lyssna — zero cost and fully owned
Learnings: Pathname-based SPA routing + Supabase anon key = zero-backend-cost platform deployable in one git push

Task: Launch A/B tests and share participant links
Comment: Submitted before Boxing class at 18:30

column1: Day 7 — A/B analysis + Fit & Finish round 1
Task: Watch all 4 participant videos with focus on A/B screen interaction — face / voice / taps
Comment: 'Doing' beats 'saying' — watch what they actually do
Learnings: Version B outperformed on all 3 tasks — see AB-Test-Summary.pdf

Task: Capture 1 key screenshot per participant showing reaction to A/B screens
Comment: Frames become evidence in the writeup and poster
Learnings: Per-participant screenshot turns verbal feedback into a concrete evidence artefact

Task: Compare task completion times + error counts + qualitative ratings between variants
Comment: Used a 2×4 hand-built table — n=4 doesn't need stats software
Learnings: 2×4 hand-built table is sufficient for directional A/B findings; no stats software needed at n=4

Task: Write up findings — which variant performed better and why; surprises vs confirmed hypothesis
Comment: Distinguish 'expected' from 'unexpected' findings clearly
Learnings: Revision-List.md with evidence quotes and alternatives per item is more actionable than a prose summary

Task: Apply top improvements from A/B + user testing results — R-02 (post-connect nav) implemented
Comment: R-02 done: post-connect back navigates to Subscriptions tab instead of Add screen
Learnings: Post-connect destination should always be the screen where the result appears — not the screen that launched the action

Task: Phase 2 A/B platform — demographics collection + split-pane task view + task timing analytics
Comment: Split-pane iframe shows prototype alongside task instructions — no tab switching needed
Learnings: Split-pane iframe embed is cleaner than asking participants to open a separate tab

Task: Redesign results dashboard — sticky header + summary comparison cards + per-task T1/T2/T3 columns + qualitative cards
Comment: Replaced aggregate bar chart with per-task per-participant breakdown
Learnings: Local token constants beat CSS var() references in a standalone React page with no shared theme context

Task: Document live URLs and A/B status in README; fill placeholder URLs in ab-test-materials.md
Comment: README is now the single entry point for all prototype and survey links
Learnings: README-as-quick-links table is more useful than a narrative description for a working prototype

Task: Polish microinteractions — button presses + transitions + focus rings + hover states (R-15)
Comment: Carried to Day 8 — polish after structural revisions land

Task: Accessibility pass — contrast 4.5:1 + visible focus rings + keyboard tab order + ARIA labels (R-14)
Comment: Carried to Day 8

Task: Apply code revisions R-01 (select-all CTA in Review Findings) + R-03 (remove Add from Home header) + R-05 (plain-language confidence label) + R-13 (screenshot placeholder)
Comment: Priority revisions from A/B + user testing — see Revision-List.md for evidence and alternatives

Task: Final design review pass — adopt Version B as default (R-04); add AI suggestions label (R-07); subscription sorting (R-10); category breakdown discoverability (R-11)
Comment: Version B is confirmed default — remove ?v=b requirement and treat as canonical

Task: Brainstorm 10 app name candidates and narrow to 1 (current name is placeholder)
Comment: Name should hint at clarity / control over recurring charges

Task: Design app icon — light + dark variants
Comment: Icon needs to read at 24px and 1024px

Task: Write tagline + short marketing description for poster and submission
Comment: One sentence that survives a 3-second scan

Task: Build project poster — Problem / Solution / Results
Comment: Three-panel structure mirrors course expectation

Task: Record 1-minute video walkthrough of key features
Comment: Demo Connect flow + Review Findings as the hero moment

Task: Prepare and rehearse 1-minute live pitch (3x runs)
Comment: Rehearse out loud — silent rehearsal undercounts time

Task: Snapshot development plan to PDF and submit
Comment: Final submission artefact

Task: Deploy final live demo URL
Comment: Vercel GitHub integration — auto-deployed on every push from Day 6 onwards
Learnings: Every commit auto-deploys — no manual deploy step needed with Vercel + GitHub

Task: STRETCH — push notifications mockup screen (renewal reminders)
Comment: Demonstrates renewal-alert system extending beyond the app

Task: STRETCH — first-time onboarding flow (3-card carousel)
Comment: Shows the value-prop walk-through new users would see

Task: STRETCH — cancellation assistance flow (link out to provider cancel pages)
Comment: Closes the loop on 'find unused subs' with action

Task: STRETCH — subtle entrance animations on key cards (fade-in on first view)
Comment: Adds polish without bloating the prototype

Task: Picsart full-time work Mon-Fri daytime — design / test windows are evenings 18:00+ and full weekends
Comment: Limits weekday focus blocks to ~3-4 hours per evening

Task: Boxing class Wed 18:30 (1h) — affects Day 6 schedule
Comment: Submit A/B tests before 18:30 so they run overnight

Task: Zumba class Thu 19:00 (1h) — affects Day 7 schedule
Comment: Front-load A/B analysis to morning / midday

Task: GTV (Global Talent Visa UK) application prep — ongoing parallel project
Comment: Visa work pulls weekend mornings

Task: Substack 2x/week + LinkedIn growth content — ongoing parallel commitments
Comment: Content production batched on Sundays
